# moodle-cli 0.4.0

## Summary

This release expands the CLI from a course-and-forum tool into a more complete URL-first Moodle workflow for agents and terminal users.

It adds direct activity detail commands, supports pasting supported Moodle URLs straight into `moodle`, exposes a shared `skills` install entry point, and lets `moodle update` upgrade the installed CLI directly.

## Highlights

### Activity detail commands

`moodle-cli` now includes dedicated commands for common Moodle activity pages:

- `moodle assignment`
- `moodle quiz`
- `moodle resource`
- `moodle link`
- `moodle page`
- `moodle folder`

Each command accepts either the Moodle module ID or the original Moodle URL and supports structured JSON and YAML output.

### URL-first routing

You can now paste supported Moodle URLs directly as the first CLI argument and let `moodle` route to the narrowest matching command.

This covers:

- forum discussion URLs
- forum view URLs
- assignment, quiz, resource, link, page, and folder URLs
- course URLs
- grade report URLs

This makes agent and shell workflows shorter because users no longer need to manually translate a Moodle link into the right subcommand.

### Skill install entry point

The CLI now exposes a shared skills installer alias:

- `moodle skills`
- `moodle skills add`

`moodle skills add` delegates to `npx skills add https://github.com/bunizao/moodle-cli` and falls back to `npm exec` when `npx` is unavailable.

### Direct self-update

`moodle update` now tries to apply the upgrade directly instead of only printing manual commands.

It prefers:

1. `uv tool upgrade moodle-cli`
2. `pipx upgrade moodle-cli`

Users who only want the old check behavior can run `moodle update --check-only`.

### More reliable version reporting

`moodle --version` now resolves the installed package version through package metadata and falls back to the local project version in source checkouts.

This makes version output and release validation more reliable across local development, CI, and packaged installs.

## User-visible changes

- Added direct activity commands for assignment, quiz, resource, link, page, and folder pages
- Added top-level URL routing for supported Moodle links
- Added `moodle skills` and `moodle skills add`
- Changed `moodle update` to perform upgrades directly by default
- Added `moodle update --check-only`
- Improved version detection for source and installed environments

## Examples

```bash
moodle "https://school.example.edu/mod/assign/view.php?id=302" --json
moodle "https://school.example.edu/mod/forum/discuss.php?d=9001#p9101" --json
moodle assignment 302 --json
moodle page "https://school.example.edu/mod/page/view.php?id=601"
moodle skills add
moodle update --check-only
```

## Validation

Validated with:

- `uv run pytest -q`
- `uv build`
- `uv run moodle --help`
- `uv run moodle update --check-only`
